description: Interactive personality quiz that matches user archetypes to product variants, encouraging self-identification and participation through relatable character comparisons.
structural_recipe: Create a visual grid or carousel showing 3-4 product variants (drinks, menu items) each paired with a distinct personality archetype. Use direct interrogative opening (أيهم أنت؟). Include visual distinction per variant (colors, emojis, descriptive labels). Add brand hashtags and community-building tagline. Prompt audience to self-select in comments or save for sharing.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences engage with content that enables self-expression and social comparison. Personality quizzes leverage intrinsic motivation to define identity and belong to a group. The pattern reduces perceived distance between consumer and brand by inviting users into a co-creative labeling exercise. Hashtags like #مننا_ويفهم_جونا reinforce tribal belonging and insider status, amplifying share and comment rates.
transplantation_caveats: Personality archetypes must reflect Saudi cultural values—avoid stereotypes around gender roles, religious observance, or social hierarchy
Quiz framing should feel playful, not judgmental; negative personality associations risk alienating segments
Ensure product variants are actually available and equally promoted to avoid stock-out friction post-engagement
cultural_constraints: Personality labels should avoid haram associations (alcohol, immodesty, irreverence toward authority)
Character descriptions must be inclusive across gender and age demographics in Saudi context
